headline: A 4.8 Percent Yield Portfolio That Lets a 72-Year-Old Sleep Through Every Quarterly Earnings Surprise
summary: The mechanics of retirement income are often simpler than the investment products marketed to provide it. A 72-year-old retiree with $1.1 million invested and an income goal of $52,800 per year, or $4,400 per month, needs a portfolio yield of roughly 4.8%. Everything else, from security selection and account placement to rebalancing decisions, revolves around ... A 4.8 Percent Yield Portfolio That Lets a 72-Year-Old Sleep Through Every Quarterly Earnings Surprise

headline: Jefferies Sees Upside in Realty Income (O) as Net Lease REIT Valuations Remain Depressed
summary: Realty Income Corporation (NYSE:O) is included among the 10 No-Brainer Dividend Stocks to Buy. On June 1, Jefferies initiated coverage of Realty Income Corporation (NYSE:O) with a Buy rating and a $69 price target, compared with its previous target of $75. headline: Without a Roth, $60,000 in Dividend Income From These 5 Stocks Means You Owe the IRS $22,200
summary: At the 37% top federal bracket, a portfolio throwing off $60,000 in non-qualified dividend income hands the IRS $22,200 every April, before state taxes and before the 3.8% net investment income tax (NIIT) surtax that also applies at this income level. That is the cost of holding REITs, BDCs and other ordinary-income dividend payers in ... Without a Roth, $60,000 in Dividend Income From These 5 Stocks Means You Owe the IRS $22,200

headline: Realty Income: A Top Income Pick And Hard Assets Are About To Shine
summary: Realty Income delivered strong Q1 2026 results, with AFFO per share and occupancy up, reinforcing its dependable income profile. See why O stock is a strong buy.
